title: Xanthophyllum lanceatum
intro: Xanthophyllum lanceatum is a plant species belonging to the family Polygalaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress-related diseases. Xanthophyllum lanceatum is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Xanthophyllum lanceatum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Xanthophyllum lanceatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae. This family is a group of plants commonly known as the milkwort family. The genus Xanthophyllum is a part of this family and is native to Southeast Asia. Xanthophyllum lanceatum specifically is native to the tropical regions of the continent. The plant is often found in shaded areas and is known for its unique characteristics within the Polygalaceae family.
